versions.label: BSB
paragraphs: 9 You are to count off seven weeks from the time you first put the sickle to the standing grain. 10 And you shall celebrate the Feast of Weeks to the LORD your God with a freewill offering that you give in proportion to how the LORD your God has blessed you, 11 and you shall rejoice before the LORD your God in the place He will choose as a dwelling for His Name—you, your sons and daughters, your menservants and maidservants, and the Levite within your gates, as well as the foreigner, the fatherless, and the widows among you.
12 Remember that you were slaves in Egypt, and carefully follow these statutes.

context_lines: In **Deuteronomy 16**, this section should be read within the flow of the chapter and the larger movement of Deuteronomy.
common_interpretation_lines: A common reading of **The Feast of Weeks** is that it should be interpreted in light of the chapter's full narrative movement rather than as an isolated proof text.
Where interpreters differ on details, the immediate setting in **Deuteronomy 16** remains the first control on meaning.
